skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: During a week-long cycling trip, the shortest daily distance Tomas rode was 35 kilometers and the longest daily distance he rode was 90 kilometers. Which inequality is true for any daily distance d, in kilometers, Tomas rode during this trip?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Because the shortest daily distance was 35 km, every daily distance d satisfies d ≥ 35. Because the longest daily distance was 90 km, every daily distance also satisfies d ≤ 90. Combining these gives the compound inequality 35 ≤ d ≤ 90.

Choice B describes only the lower bound and reverses its direction. Choice C describes only the upper bound and reverses its direction. Choice D uses the sum of the two endpoints, which is not a meaningful bound here.
